latest.title: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
18 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7356I
Luceco PLC
18 June 2026
18 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
APPOINTMENT OF NON-EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR
The Board of Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group"), a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems, is pleased to announce the appointment of Andrew Mines as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective from 1 August 2026.
Andrew is an experienced FTSE 100 executive who is currently Managing Director of the Electric Thermal Solutions division of Spirax Group plc, the global provider of thermal energy and fluid technology solutions that is playing a critical role in enabling the transition to net zero. He joined Spirax in 2019, initially as Managing Director of Watson-Marlow Fluid Technology Solutions business.
Andrew brings broad international industrial experience, having previously spent 23 years at Illinois Tool Works Inc (ITW), the S&P 500 global multi-industrial manufacturer. He was latterly Executive Vice President, Global Construction Products, where he was also a member of the ITW's executive leadership team.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"We are delighted to welcome Andrew to the Board. He brings excellent international industry experience with notable expertise in manufacturing of net zero enabling products and solutions. This aligns strongly with the structural growth opportunities from electrification that Luceco's competitive advantages position us perfectly to capture."
The Company makes this announcement in accordance with UKLR 6.4.6. There are no other matters to be disclosed under UKLR 6.4.8.
Luceco plc
Contact
Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer
Sodali & Co
Contact
James White
Pete Lambie
Tilly Abraham
078 5543 2699 / 079 3535 1934
Note to Editors
Luceco plc (LSE:LUCE) is a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems. The Group designs and manufactures its market-leading range of wiring accessories, EV chargers, LED lighting, and portable power products at its state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, distributing them through professional, wholesale and retail channels.
For more information, please visit
www.lucecoplc.com
.

latest.title: CEO Retirement
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2774I
Luceco PLC
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
CEO RETIREMENT
Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group") announces that, following 21 years of leading the business, John Hornby has decided to retire from the Group, with effect from 31 December 2026.
John will be taking a sabbatical until late August 2026, and will remain as Chief Executive Officer and as a Board Director until 31 December, following which he will be available to the Group in an advisory capacity. During John's sabbatical, the Group's Senior Leadership Team, supported by the Board, will discharge the functions and responsibilities of the CEO.
The Board has commenced the process of identifying a permanent successor and will make a further announcement in due course.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank John for his longstanding contribution to Luceco. He has been an important part of Luceco's development, expanding the Group significantly into the successful business it is today, with a proven platform for growth. The Board and Luceco colleagues wish John the very best for the future."
John Hornby, commented:
"It has been a privilege to serve as Chief Executive Officer of Luceco and I am enormously proud of the Group's development over this period. With a strong platform for growth, underpinned by Luceco's competitive advantages and structural opportunities, the Group is confident in the prospects for the current financial year and beyond."
The trading momentum described in the Group's Q1 Trading Update published on 19 May 2026 has been sustained through Q2-to-date. The outlook for the year ending December 2026 remains consistent with the commentary provided in that announcement and accordingly, the Board continues to expect Adjusted Operating Profit to exceed £40m, with the potential for further significant outperformance dependent on Demand Flexibility. A half year trading update will be announced towards the end of July 2026.
Details of John Hornby's remuneration arrangements in connection with his retirement will be set out in the Section 430(2B) disclosure that will be made available on Luceco's website in due course, and will also be set out in the Remuneration Report.
Luceco plc
Contact
Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer

company_30s.what_they_do: Luceco specialises in designing, manufacturing and installing lighting solutions for commercial and industrial sites. Their products include LED panels, track lighting and smart control systems that help businesses cut energy costs and improve visual quality.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Lighting is a $200 billion global market that is steadily shifting toward energy‑efficient, connected fixtures. Luceco’s expertise in modular LED technology gives it a foothold in a sector that is expanding as businesses upgrade to greener, smarter infrastructure.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a restructuring phase after a CEO exit and a change in board composition. It is still generating revenue but has been experiencing a cash burn cycle while it realigns its product roadmap and sales strategy. The share price sits far below its historical peak, indicating a steep decline but also a potential entry point for a turnaround.

rns_narrative: The most recent filings show a new non‑executive director and a CEO retirement, signalling a leadership refresh aimed at stabilising governance. The AGM results reveal modest profitability, and the company has taken a market purchase of shares to support liquidity.

Two key themes emerge: (1) governance overhaul – new directors and a fresh CEO to bring credibility, and (2) cash management – the share buy‑back and EBT purchase suggest the firm is tightening its balance sheet while keeping the business afloat.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is cautiously hopeful that fresh leadership will unlock value.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are pointing to the leadership change and the company’s core lighting business as a catalyst for a rebound. They believe the new board will drive cost cuts and push the product line into high‑margin smart lighting.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the CEO exit and board shake‑up are symptoms of deeper problems – weak sales, high debt, and a crowded market that could squeeze margins further.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment is shifting from indifference to mild optimism, but still muted. The lack of widespread chatter means there is still plenty of room for surprise if a positive announcement surfaces.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest is currently driving the stock. This silence is a green light for a contrarian play because it suggests the market has not yet priced in a potential recovery.

near_term_catalysts: A clear earnings beat or a new contract win that pushes revenue above the 12‑month average would signal that the business model is working.
A public announcement of a partnership with a major smart‑building provider could validate the company’s move into connected lighting.
A volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average while the price touches a historical support level would confirm that buyers are returning.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on the next quarterly report for any sign of improving gross margin and on the board meeting minutes for evidence of cost‑cutting measures being implemented. A sudden drop in the debt‑to‑equity ratio would also strengthen the bullish case.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the early stages of a recovery from a deep sell‑off. A sharp drop in price combined with a flat volume base is typical of capitulation. The APEX score (high) indicates tight compression – a classic setup for a mean‑reversion move.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock recovers to the midpoint of its previous high, the upside is roughly 40% from the current level – a 4:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio. The RED‑style crash profile, if confirmed, historically delivers 160%+ returns on the best bounces, so the upside potential is significant.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The worst case is that the company fails to stabilise its earnings, leading to a further 20–30% decline before a potential delisting. The downside remains capped at the current trough, but the risk of prolonged stagnation is real.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose up to the 30% drop that brought the share to its current level, but you stand to gain 120% if the price climbs to the mid‑point of its historic peak. The asymmetry is clearly in favour of the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average, size 5–10% of your discretionary capital, and set a stop 5% below that average. Use a trailing stop once the price moves 10% above entry to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume surge of at least 1.5× the 20‑day average and the price touches the 50‑day moving average. That would be a strong confirmation that buyers are returning.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings have been reported.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Luceco’s deep price plunge, coupled with a quiet market and a fresh board, creates a contrarian play where a modest recovery could deliver a 4:1 reward‑to‑risk payoff.
